steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Point P = (3, −5). Test whether it satisfies Inequality I ($y < -3x + 7$), Inequality II ($y > x - 5$), or both.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Inequality I — $y < -3x + 7$: Substitute (3, −5): $-5 < -3(3) + 7 = -2$. Since $-5 < -2$, this is TRUE. ✓
  • Inequality II — $y > x - 5$: Substitute (3, −5): $-5 > 3 - 5 = -2$. Since $-5 > -2$ is false, this is FALSE. ✗
P satisfies only Inequality I — that matches choice A. A is correct.
